description.task: Implement a hash table from scratch using an array of linked lists (separate chaining) for collision resolution. Support put, get, remove, and size operations. Use a simple modulo hash function.
instructions: remove is already implemented — just the put needs completion
Keys 1, 9, 17 all hash to 1 (mod 8) — demonstrating chaining handles collisions
description.expectedOutput:
get(9): nine
get(17): seventeen
size: 5
after remove(9), get(9): null
size: 4

description.task: Build a hash table that tracks its load factor (size / capacity). When the load factor exceeds 0.75, rehash: create a new table with double the capacity and re-insert all entries. Print the load factor before and after rehashing.
instructions: With initial capacity=4 and threshold=0.75: first rehash at size=4 (load=1.0>0.75? — actually at 4 elements after insert since 4/4=1.0>0.75)
After putting 3 elements: load=3/4=0.75 (not > threshold), put 4th: load=4/4=1.0>0.75 → rehash to capacity=8
Second rehash when load exceeds 0.75 again at capacity 8
The rehash method is already implemented — just add the condition in put
description.expectedOutput:
Initial capacity: 4
put(1): size=1 cap=4 load=0.25
put(2): size=2 cap=4 load=0.50
put(3): size=3 cap=4 load=0.75
Rehashing: capacity 4 -> 8 (load=1.00)
put(4): size=4 cap=8 load=0.50
put(5): size=5 cap=8 load=0.63
put(6): size=6 cap=8 load=0.75
Rehashing: capacity 8 -> 16 (load=0.88)
put(7): size=7 cap=16 load=0.44
put(8): size=8 cap=16 load=0.50
get(5): 50

description.task: Use Java's HashMap to solve classic frequency-counting problems: (1) count word frequencies in a sentence, (2) find the first non-repeating character, (3) detect duplicate values in an array.
instructions: firstNonRepeating: for(char c:s.toCharArray()) count.merge(c,1,Integer::sum); then for(char c:s.toCharArray()) if(count.get(c)==1) return c;
hasDuplicate: for(int x:arr){if(!seen.add(x))return true;} return false;
First non-repeat in 'aabbcde': c; in 'aabb': \0 (all repeating)
description.hint: wordFrequency: freq.merge(w,1,Integer::sum); firstNonRepeating: use LinkedHashMap to preserve insertion order; hasDuplicate: if(!seen.add(x))return true;
description.expectedOutput:
{the=3, cat=2, sat=1, on=1, mat=1}
First non-repeat in 'aabbcde': c
First non-repeat in 'aabb':    �
Has duplicate [1,2,3,4]: false
Has duplicate [1,2,3,1]: true
